ordered_transfer: complete the final memory map and successful ExitBootServices call
prove zero firmware calls after successful ExitBootServices
validate the opt-in QEMU-only development transfer envelope
emit the transfer-arm and one-way development boundary markers
disable interrupts and clear the direction flag
install the retained PKMAP2 CR3 while preserving only permitted PWT/PCD bits
place PBP1 virtual address, exact byte count, and PBP1 magic in RDI, RSI, and RDX
place the validated ordinary-transfer trap selector zero in R10
jump once to the PBP1-bound PooleKernel entry virtual address
capture CR3 and RFLAGS in the assembly entry wrapper
revalidate transfer continuity and reject the authenticated production profile
independently reparse nine retained files under PKREVAL1
emit identical serial and debugcon evidence
halt permanently after the unsigned policy denial

non_claims: The ordinary PooleBoot build still stops before transfer; live dispatch requires the explicit development-transfer feature.
The PKXFER1 unsigned terminal-denial receipt accepts trap selector zero only; nonzero QEMU scenarios are qualified separately by PKTRAP1.
The exited-development PBP1 intentionally fails the authenticated production PKENTRY1 profile.
The live kernel path grants no capability, lifecycle, recovery, symbol, microcode, firmware, policy, or state-write authority.
The framebuffer still uses inherited firmware translation and cache attributes pending final remap and revocation work.
No target firmware, physical hardware, second independent host, physical-media write, signed release, ISO, installer, or production promotion is claimed.
Owner authorization for signing or privileged operations does not substitute for the missing qualification evidence or safety gates.
